pi_items.verified_value: All keywords are present in USLCI database processes for natural gas electricity generation. USLCI contains processes like 'Electricity, natural gas, at power plant' which involve natural gas combustion for power/electricity generation.
pi_items.source: USLCI database documentation; openLCA wiki; web search results for USLCI natural gas electricity; verifier.py line 115 uses similar keywords for validation

pi_items.verified_value: TRACI 2.1 includes a Global Warming Potential (GWP) / Climate Change impact category. GWP uses IPCC AR5 100-year characterization factors for greenhouse gases. All keywords are standard terminology for this impact category in TRACI 2.1.
pi_items.source: TRACI 2.1 documentation; EPA TRACI tool page; Figshare dataset 25782786 description; EPD Guide for TRACI 2.1; install_openlca.sh line 201-203

privileged_info_summary: The hotspot_contribution_analysis task uses two real, publicly available life cycle assessment datasets: the USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory) Database FY24 Q4 release from the Federal LCA Commons, which contains hundreds of processes including electricity generation from natural gas combustion; and TRACI 2.1 (Tool for the Reduction and Assessment of Chemicals and Other Environmental Impacts), version 2.1, published by the U.S. EPA, which includes a Global Warming Potential (GWP) impact category using IPCC AR5 characterization factors. Both datasets are sourced from authoritative U.S. government repositories (NREL/FLCAC for USLCI and USDA/EPA for TRACI 2.1). The task expects to find processes containing keywords related to natural gas electricity generation (natural gas, electricity, power, combustion) and to calculate impacts in categories related to climate change (global warming, GWP, climate change, greenhouse). The task is synthetic as an automated benchmark, but operates on real production LCA data.
